Runner Runner (2013)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $19,316,646Details
International Box Office $41,196,034
Worldwide Box Office $60,512,680
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $3,814,599
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $2,271,703
Total $6,086,302
Further financial details...

Synopsis

When Princeton grad student Richie Furst believes he’s been swindled by an online poker site, he heads to Costa Rica to confront gambling tycoon Ivan Block, the man he thinks is responsible. But Richie will soon face the ultimate power play as he finds himself caught between Block’s promises of unlimited wealth and the zealous FBI agent trying to bring him down.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$7,706,712 (39.9% of total gross)
Legs:2.51 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:31.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$30,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.0 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,026 opening theaters/3,026 max. theaters, 3.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $28,416,613

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 4th, 2013 (Wide) by 20th Century Fox
International Releases: September 26th, 2013 (Wide) (Serbia and Montenegro)
September 26th, 2013 (Wide) (Ukraine)
September 27th, 2013 (Wide) (Bulgaria)
September 27th, 2013 (Wide) (Kenya)
September 27th, 2013 (Wide) (Turkey)
Video Release: January 7th, 2014 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for language and some sexual content.
(Rating bulletin 2276, 6/12/2013)
Running Time: 91 minutes
Filming location (country): United States
Plot point: Blackmail, FBI, Gambling, Internet, Revenge
Subgenre: Crime Thriller
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: New Regency, Appian Way, Double Feature Films, Regency Enterprises
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

When Princeton grad student Richie Furst believes he’s been swindled by an online poker site, he heads to Costa Rica to confront gambling tycoon Ivan Block, the man he thinks is responsible. But Richie will soon face the ultimate power play as he finds himself caught between Block’s promises of unlimited wealth and the zealous FBI agent trying to bring him down.
